__comment: Finance Tracker — RED Method Dashboard (Rate, Errors, Duration)

__explanation.what_is_this: A Grafana dashboard definition in JSON. Grafana reads this file and renders panels with live data from Prometheus. Each panel is a PromQL query that fetches metric data and visualizes it.

red_method.Rate: How many requests per second is the API handling right now?
red_method.Errors: What percentage of those requests are failing (5xx)?
red_method.Duration: How long are requests taking? (P50, P95, P99 latencies)

__explanation.user_story: As an on-call engineer, I open this dashboard and within 10 seconds I know: is the system healthy? If not, is it slow or erroring? Which endpoint is the problem?

description: Finance Tracker API — Request Rate, Error Rate, and Latency (RED Method)

panels.title: R — Rate (Requests per Second)
panels.__explanation: Section header — groups the rate panels together visually.

panels.description:
Total HTTP requests per second across all endpoints and status codes.
Higher is more traffic. Sudden drop to 0 means service is down.

defaults.__unit_explanation: reqps = requests per second. Grafana automatically formats the Y-axis.

__expr_explanation.rate(http_requests_total[5m]): Per-second rate of the counter over the last 5 minutes. rate() handles counter resets (process restarts).
__expr_explanation.sum(...) by (method, endpoint): Group by method (GET/POST) and endpoint path. One line per unique combination.
__expr_explanation.result: A time series line for each endpoint. You can see which endpoints get the most traffic.

targets.__legendFormat_explanation: Label template — each line in the legend shows the method and endpoint name.

panels.title: Request Rate by Endpoint
panels.__type_explanation: timeseries = line chart with time on X-axis, values on Y-axis.

panels.description: Overall requests per second — one single number showing current traffic volume.

thresholds.__thresholds_explanation: Green below 50 req/s, yellow 50-100, red above 100. Thresholds depend on your infrastructure capacity.

options.__reduceOptions_explanation: Show the MOST RECENT non-null value — the current state, not a summary.

panels.title: Current Request Rate
panels.__type_explanation: stat = single large number with color coding. Good for 'what is the value RIGHT NOW?'

panels.description:
Breakdown of responses by HTTP status code family.
2xx = success, 4xx = client errors (bad request, auth), 5xx = server errors (bugs, crashes).

targets.__expr_explanation: status_code=~"2.." is a regex match. ~= means regex. 2.. matches 200, 201, 204, etc.

panels.title: Requests by Status Code

panels.description:
Percentage of requests returning 5xx errors over the last 5 minutes.
SLO: keep below 0.1% (99.9% success rate).
Alert fires at 1%.

targets.__expr_explanation: Fraction of 5xx responses × 100 to get a percentage. If 5 out of 100 requests fail, this = 5.

panels.title: Error Rate (5xx %)
panels.__type_explanation: gauge = speedometer-style panel. The needle shows the current value. Thresholds color the arcs.

panels.description: Error rate trend over time. Useful for seeing if errors are getting better or worse.

targets.__expr_explanation: > bool 1 returns 1 when above threshold, 0 otherwise. Combined with threshold coloring, this shows the SLO breach line.

panels.title: Error Rate Over Time

panels.description:
P50, P95, P99 latency across ALL endpoints.
P50 = median (half of requests are faster).
P95 = 95th percentile (1 in 20 requests is slower).
P99 = 99th percentile (1 in 100 requests is slower) — most important for SLOs.

__expr_explanation.histogram_quantile(0.50, ...): Compute the 50th percentile from the histogram buckets.
__expr_explanation._bucket suffix: Auto-generated by prometheus_client for Histogram objects. Each bucket is a counter of requests that completed in <= that time.
__expr_explanation.sum(...) by (le): le = 'less than or equal'. The bucket label. Must keep le for histogram_quantile to work.

panels.title: Latency Percentiles (All Endpoints)

panels.description:
P99 latency right now — the single most important number for SLO compliance.
Green = under 500ms (SLO met). Red = over 500ms (SLO breached).
panels.title: P99 Latency (Current)

panels.description:
P99 latency broken down by endpoint. Shows which specific route is slow.
Example: /transactions/ is slow (event replay) but /accounts/ is fast (projection table + cache).

targets.__expr_explanation: Same P99 calculation but keeping the 'endpoint' label. Result: one line per endpoint. Tells you WHERE the slowness is.

panels.title: P99 Latency by Endpoint

panels.description:
Rate of 429 responses per second. High = users being throttled.
Could indicate: DDoS, buggy client, or rate limit set too low.
panels.title: Rate Limiting — 429s per Second

panels.description:
Percentage of requests that get 429 rate-limited.
Above 5% = alert fires. Normal = near 0%.
panels.title: Rate Limit Rejection Rate %

__refresh_explanation: Dashboard auto-refreshes every 10 seconds — live view during incidents.

list.name: interval
list.__type_explanation: A dropdown variable. Change to 1m for fine-grained view during incident, 1h for trend analysis.

__time_explanation: Default time range: last 1 hour. Change to 'Last 24 hours' for post-incident review.
title: Finance Tracker — RED Method Dashboard
__uid_explanation: Stable UID — Grafana uses this to de-duplicate dashboards. Same UID = update existing, not create duplicate.
